reviews.title: TERRIBLY a scam
reviews.text: We arrived at the hotel at around 9am and I asked Liza if she could allow us to use Thier bathroom/toilet for us to refreshen since our check in time was 1pm and she allowed us to used the second floor toilet. My kids were the first to use the bathroom but they came out immediately after seeing the toilet/bathroom. When I checked oMG the toilet bowl was very dark,it seems they are not cleaning it as well as the floor tiles are very dark brown. My kids described it small and dirty and there was unflush poops. When we came no one was there and we had the difficulties of bringing our luggages at the second floor since there's no lift/elevator. The receiving area  was delapidated and unclean.  We decided to transfer at Mercury hotel and still paid Liza our hotel accommodation without discount even if we didn't use the room. So sad what you see here in Agoda is not the reality. Don't book here it's a waste of money. We are on vacation and we deserve a better place for our staycation. reviews.title: Unclean Room, No Free Water, but Good Staff Support
reviews.text:
The staff at the hotel were friendly and helpful — I really appreciated the motorbike rental and one-day trip services they offered. However, the cleanliness of the room seriously needs improvement. I stayed for 3 nights and the room was never cleaned, nor were the blankets changed. Both my companion and I experienced intense itching and developed red spots on our skin, which felt like insect bites or a possible reaction to dust. I have plenty of photos to support this.

Another disappointing point: drinking water was not provided for free. Unlike cheaper hotels I’ve stayed at before, they charged 10,000 per bottle, which I found unreasonable.

What was even more frustrating was that the hotel staff came into our room every day while we were out — not to clean, but to check whether we took any water or left the air conditioning on. Honestly, I would have preferred they used that time to clean the room instead.

reviews.title: Terrible
reviews.text:
It was different compared to what we booked. The room we were given was cheaper than the one we booked and just realized that after I started comparing the rooms..
We arrived early but checked in at 1pm but we understand that.. the host helped us carrying the suitcase as she knows how difficult it could be to go up on a narrow staircase till 5th floor.
The room anyway was not clean, tho it is a 2 star homestay.. hair in bed, very old towels with mold stains, mold on the wall, the ceiling fan was white but now it is black.. a deep clean of the room would’t hurt…
What if someone with mold allergies get that room? 
There was still a sim card on the floor with powders next to our bed..
And unfortunately when we were there, there was a construction ongoing and the problem was they started working at midnight so it was noisy in the night..
I wanted to complain but anyway I think all their rooms are in that situation. So we just decided to just check out days before instead of staying longer… 
It was a terrible stay..
And the fact that the 3 nights not used.. still had to pay for the 3 bottle of water we had in the room… 

If you are okay with this for the 2 stars and amount of money then go for it otherwise I won’t recommend it cause the stars goes with the facilities that it offers but the cleanliness is important.

reviews.title: Nothing was quite right
reviews.text: Everything about this homestay was a problem from the start. First, the room we booked was not available so we were put in a less nice room for one night and told we had to move to a different room for the second night. The second room was nicer but still not ideal. The cleanliness was ok aside from the laundry service which was a disaster. Our clothes were extremely dirty upon return so we brought to another place for washing. We did not have to pay for the service thankfully. The hot water for shower was limited and there was no drinking water available except for purchase for high price so we did not use. The kettle to boil water was moldy. The wifi did not work in our first room but worked ok in our second room. The staff are trying their best and the location is really good but I may look elsewhere for a better option for similar price.

reviews.title: Not ideal
reviews.text: Here’s the deal: I’d stay here if (a) you’re in a desperate situation or (b) you require only the bare minimum. AC worked and shower had hot water. But the room itself had no windows, was pretty dingy, with stains on sheets, light in bathroom stopped working during stay, and no hand soap. It met our bare needs but we wouldn’t stay again. I found the photos to be deceptive. Nice aspects: location, it’s above a pho restaurant which lends a nice charm and the staff member at “reception” was kind and tried to communicate (despite no English). Wasn't a big deal since we aren’t light sleepers but paper thin walls (could clearly hear Vietnamese family in neighboring room/house).

The ac/heater is working too. There was a construction nearby so it is noisy. I can also hear a washing machine nearby. There is no dining table, so I eat on the bed. Overall it’s an ok place to rent if you just want somewhere to leave your things and have a private bathroom and clean bed.

reviews.text:
Pros:
-Location
-Budget hotel
-Staff is nice and kind
-There is a travel and tours company below the hotel so if you plan to book some tours it'll be easier.

Cons:
-Narrow stairs going to the room and hotel. You'll have difficulties carrying your large luggage.
-The room is old and you can see stains from the walls and ceiling
-Room is gloomy and sad due to dim and poor lightings.
-Bedsheets, pillowcases, blankets and towels have stains and doesn't smell clean.
-The toilet and the bedroom only have a wall partition. You can easily smell and hear what business someone is doing in the bathroom. 

Overall, this is a good hotel given that it is cheap and has a very nice location. The staff is really nice too.

reviews.title: Overall Good
reviews.text:
The location is superb! It is located at the center of the old quarter wherein almost all of the tourist spots in Hanoi are just walking distance. Just 2 blocks away from the night market and the Hoan Kiem Lake and a lot of coffeeshops nearby, banh mi and pho. 

Location-wise and the price is worth it. Just do not expect it to be that luxurious because this looks like just an old house. But still since it is very afforable and location is great, I would still recommend this for budget travellers like us!

reviews.title: Evaluation for 2 days, 2 nights break.
reviews.text:
The location in the old town is convenient, very close to central dining spots.  
The room amenities are a bit outdated, sometimes there is a water outage, and the air conditioning leaks water into the room.  
The staff, especially the room attendant, is a bit older and slow, forgetting towels and turning on the pump for guests.  
The price is good.  
Overall, I might stay here again because the location is very convenient in the heart of Hanoi.

reviews.title: Value for money

reviews.title: Very good
reviews.text: Adjust your expectations.

reviews.title: My trip to hanoi
reviews.text:
Excellent location in a very typical Vietnamese building, narrow and tall with a restaurant at street level & residential above . I got a room with queen bed & balcony over looking the street . It’s close enough to many tourists sites & far enough to be quiet at night . It’s basic but clean enough. 
The staff is very responsive & available when needed. 
I would recommend this place & the price is reasonable too

reviews.title: Kind staff
reviews.text: She’s doing everything alone can’t speak English but she used google translate to understand us and she sooo sweet

reviews.title: Great Stay with Minor Flaws
reviews.text: The location is fantastic, making it easy to explore the area. The staff were incredibly friendly and helpful, which really enhanced the experience. However, cleanliness could use some improvement, and while the facilities were decent, they didn't quite meet expectations. Also, there was no lift, as expected in a homestay. Overall, it offers good value for money, especially considering its prime spot and the excellent service provided by the team.

reviews.title: host friendly
reviews.text: The owner is friendly, the room is neat as seen in the video and photos, but there is another room belonging to my friend that has a lot of ants, so it depends on the room, the price is according to the room you get. Overall, the price of the room is according to what you pay, no more, no less. There is no elevator, so you have to take the stairs.

reviews.title: Ok
reviews.text: Ok

reviews.title: Average but Not upto the expectations
reviews.text:
First of all, Homestay experience was on a lower side compared to the overall experience. 
Surrounding are great but this stay starts from the first floor which has a reception desk. Then there are 2 rooms on each floor upto 4th floor. Mine is on forth floor. And stairs are quite narrow, allows only one man to pass . If you have a large trolly bag then it will be very difficult to move it, same was happened to me. Room is spacious and well decorated but on 4 floor. Moisture walls was hidden under decorated wallpapers.  Water bottle in the room are also chargeable.  Scooty rented from the homestay was also costly.  Lisa the owner of the hotel, is a good lady. She is responsive.

reviews.title: A lot to improve on
reviews.text: Please check which floor you would be assigned to, you wouldn't want to carry your heavy luggage up to 4th floor. The heater and hair blower during our time was not really working. Though I suggest this room for tourists who wouldn't do anything else than sleep in the room (and those who have small budget). I can say it was comfortable but there's a lot of aspect to improve on. The staff tho are super kind, they carried our luggage up our floor at first and are very approachable.

reviews.title: Comfortable and quiet
reviews.text: Despite it being on a busy street, our room was comfortable, quiet, and clean. Location is very convenient as well since it's near an ATM, there's an eatery on the ground floor, beside a bakery, and near other establishments. Will recommend to my family and friends

reviews.text: Just few step away from Strain Street, st Josept cathedral , Hoan lake kiem.

reviews.title: A cosy nice Hotel
reviews.text: This is a very nice cozy Hotel, Owner was really nice, A bit old but its still okay especially for the price.  I just got a problem with my shower I think it was grounded but they sort it out. Water is not strong but it has hot water.  Alot of food places nearby.

reviews.title: 3 Nights
reviews.text: There’s no elevator in here. The room where we stayed has a bathroom with no ceiling which we didn’t like because if you poop, your friends will smell and hear it too, and it’s very uncomfortable. The AC is too small for the room- it takes time for the room to cool down. There’s a wall fan though, and you can just turn both AC and fan at the same time to let the cool air circulate easier. I also don’t recommend to do laundry here at the hotel because the clothes are’t properly washed off. The good thing I like here is that first water bottle is free, the CR is clean even if there’s no ceiling, and the beds and sheets are comfy. Good for budget 👌🏻

reviews.title: Excellent
reviews.text: We stayed in the family room with a balcony, and the room had a retro vibe. We loved the atmosphere, which truly reflected the liveliness of the Old Quarter. The owner, Lisa, and her sister were both helpful and accommodating—Lisa even arranged a taxi for our airport drop-off. The room was basic but clean, and you’re really paying for the fantastic location. Everything is within walking distance, making it great for exploring. Simply sitting on the balcony with a coffee from the café next door is an experience in itself—a must-do!It can be noisy for light sleepers, but that’s expected in the Old Quarter, as the location is close to the night market and surrounded by bustling food stalls and shops that never sleep.There is no lift in the property, so keep in mind that you’ll need to carry your bags. However, the owner’s sister kindly helped us take our luggage to the room.

reviews.title: Excellent
reviews.text:
Everything was good except there was no elevetor when we arrived . We had a big suitcase with us and our room was on the 4th floor. 

We did inform we arrived early in the morning from SaPa, but when we arrived no one was there. We waited for 30-45minutes until an elderly couple who is not the owner come and show us a button to open the roller shutter. After that we decided to book another hotel.
reviews.response:

But overall, we enjoyed the stay and the room is complete with all we need. They provided water in the fridge tho its not free. You get what you paid for.

However, the bathroom smelled bad, like piss. The smell didn't go away in any of the three days stay. We had a room with balcony facing to the street and it could get really noisy. 
For the price it was good tho.

reviews.title: Wouldn't recommend
reviews.text: Staff friendly and owner helpful. Found the place fine but the first  ight I didn't get to sleep untill 4am and was awake at 6 so it was hard to enjoy my first day in Hanoi! The room was at the front and it was so load all I could hear was beeping and people constantly! Even ear plugs didn't cover it! The bed was so hard it was like sleeping on concrete. I mentioned to the owner and she responded quickly and said she would move me to a quieter room which I was very grateful for. The next room was quiet and bed was so much better. Appreciated the quick response. Location was good in the middle of the quarter so easy to find everything. Kitchen barley had utensils to make food so needs updating. They charge for water! The owner told me it was because its a cheap homestay. But I have been traveling 3 months and never had to pay for water and the price has been the same! Stay was ok but didn't feel very comfortable. Deffinalty won't be staying again.

reviews.text: The staff at this homestay was incredibly helpful and accommodating. The location is perfect, right in the heart of Hanoi’s Old Quarter. reviews.title: Charming Hotel
reviews.text:
The family room is spacious, Instagram-worthy, and gives you the authentic feel of staying in a traditional Vietnamese-style house. Despite the very affordable price, the room is well-equipped with all the essentials—blow dryer, hot water, heater, toothbrush, toiletries, and slippers, which was a pleasant surprise.

The lady at the reception, though her English is limited, was incredibly warm, caring, and accommodating. We really appreciated her kindness!

Of course, given the price, don’t expect luxury, but the value for money is outstanding.

The only downside is the lack of an elevator and the steep stairs leading to the rooms. It can be quite challenging if you have large luggage, so this hotel is best suited for those traveling with small bags. Just be aware of the stairs and prepare accordingly.

Overall, we had a great stay and would recommend this hotel for its charm and affordability.

reviews.title: Highly recommended.
reviews.text: The location is very good, the size is just right, the price is cheap, and it's clean. It's close to everywhere (very close places are very lively => bar street and Sword Lake). There is a small balcony (but the balcony has no table or chairs). The only downside is that there is no refrigerator (the landlord said it was broken and being repaired, but there is a refrigerator available in the second-floor reception area). Highly recommended!

reviews.title: Cost-performance ratio is very high.

The room decor is also nice, and the room we stayed in had a balcony with a street view. The bed was very comfortable. The staff were very friendly and greeted us with a smile every day, ready to help resolve any issues promptly.

reviews.title: The cost-performance ratio is high, but the facilities are inevitably a bit old.
reviews.text:
The location is excellent, and the price is very cheap.  
The mattress is very thick and comfortable to sleep on, and there is hot water; however, it took us two days to find out that the switch for the water heater is outside the door. 😂😂  

As long as you can accept the following few drawbacks, it's a choice worth considering.  
Firstly, the door lock requires you to call the landlord to lock it; actually, we wanted to change the lock the next day, but they wouldn't replace it.  
The landlord's sister is downstairs 24/7. The facilities are average, but the service is pretty good; however, they will come in with a spare key to turn off the air conditioning and fan.  
Also, the hairdryer caught fire while we were using it, and it takes three to four hours for the air conditioning to be effective, but you can obtain a large fan from the landlord. 😂😂😂

reviews.title: Center of Old Quarter
reviews.text: We just walked everywhere from here. The staff were nice and accommodating, we slept here before our Ha Giang tour, and came back to the same homestay for 2 more nights. The room had no bugs during our stay, esp roaches so kudos to them for keeping it clean. On the room on the lower floors though--we woke up to the screaming/crying of the neighbor's child for about 30 minutes or so, so there's that. There's also a Pho store at the ground floor that's sold out by noon, so you might want to try that. They also offered to assist us with our laundry for 40K VND/kg. Very nice stay overall.

reviews.title: Better than the price
reviews.text: For the price, this room was great. We’ve stayed at other hotels at the same rate and this one was great value for the cost. The checkin staff was very friendly and let us drop our bags off early and leave them after check out until our bus departure. Water was hot and the bed was comfortable. It was a little difficult to find as you have to go inside the Pho restaurant downstairs to access it.

reviews.title: Central location, wonderful accommodation
reviews.text:
I, together with a friend, stayed here for 7 days (and 6 nights). Sunny Homestay boasts of its strategic location, and is positioned right in the heart of Old Quarter making the different cafe and renowned shops just within walking distsnce.

The building may be quite old but has a certain charm to it! The room that was provided to us was spacious and well-ventilated, and had a private toilet & shower as well. The staff had been very friendly and accommodating ❤️

For its price, you do really get your money's worth (even more). Overall, it was a comfortable and peaceful stay and one that I could very much recommend to travelers.

reviews.title: Excellent
reviews.text: Un petit hôtel sympa au cœur du vieux Hanoï. A cause de quelques soucis notre hôte a dû nous mettre dans une autre chambre, à l'étage que nous n'avons pas aimé. Mais la dernière nuit nous l'avons passé dans la chambre que nous avions réservé et celle ci est bien mieux. Elles mériteraient un peu coup de rafraîchissement, déco mais sinon elles sont bien. Le balcon était cool. C'est forcément un peu bruyant mais en plein vieux Hanoï c'est normal. Nous avons beaucoup aimé notre hôte, Lisa via WhatsApp est toujours disponible c'est vraiment top et elle parle bien anglais. Par compte, notre hôte qui est là tout le temps sur place ne parle pas anglais, juste vietnamien mais elle est tellement adorable, souriante, nous avons beaucoup aimé cette femme.

reviews.title: Hotel très bien situé et configuré pour une famille
reviews.text:
Emplacement au top
Chambre très bien configuré avec coin enfants et parents.
Il faudrait redonner un petit de neuf aux murs mais équipement très bien.
Gros point noir de cet hotel est son accessibilité: chambres au 3ieme étage avec couloir très étroit. Très difficile pour porter les grosses valises.
Malheureusement dans le vieux quartier de hanoi j'ai peur que tous soient comme ça. Personnel très charmant et avenant.

reviews.title: Très bien
reviews.text:
Logement un peu vieillot mais parfait pour un séjour court.
Nous sommes resté 2 nuits et le 1er matin fût un peu difficile car il y avait des travaux chez les voisins à partir de 7h, on avait l'impression qu'un marteau piqueur était dans notre chambre
 A part ça tout est nickel, nous sommes arrivés vers minuit et l'hotesse nous attendait et nous a accueillie avec un grand sourire. L'emplacement est assez centrale, nous avons donc pu faire le tour du vieux quartier en une journée.

reviews.response:
Dear Wen  .
Thank you for taking the time to review our property, your comments are very much valued. We appreciate your comments regarding our customer service and ongoing upgrades. We always try our best to provide only the best in customer service, and we are pleased that you found value and hospitality during your stay. We look forward to seeing you on your next trip in Hanoi! 
Best Regards, 
Lisa Thanh Nguyen

reviews.title: Homestay in the city center
reviews.text:
The location is good in the center of old quarter area..
Our room was quiet good and enough space for 5 person (2 bedroom family room), unfortunately our bedroom in the fifth floor without elevator and very small stairs,so it will be difficult if you bring a lugagge.
